Swiftwind  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Slashing Atk Delay: 21
DMG: 13 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +15 DEX: +5 STA: +10 HP: +50
SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5 SV COLD: +5 SV MAGIC: +5 SV POISON: +5
Attack: +30 Haste: +40%
WT: 2.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: RNG
Race: HUM ELF HEF HFL DRK
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

[Quests | Recipes | Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.

Quest Name

Ok, first of all, unless you are super uber (Time equipped or weapons close to those) Swiftwind is probably the best secondary weapon for a ranger. By using your OLoS, your losing about 20% haste as well as 30 atk. If I was you, I'd get rid of the OLoS and the SCHW, because to me, they don't compare to a Swiftwind.


Knaas

Have fiaqnlly achieved 1/2 of my epic!
QuoteReply
# Jul 30 2004 at 9:38 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1660522686317
Scholar
166 posts
Greeting rangers of Norath. Just wanting to let everyone know that I am now the owner of this fine sword, grats to IA for the help.

Now, I am having some real problems. Pre-epic I was using a tox axe in main with an ornate long sword of slaughter in the off hand, deadly combo. I was excited to get my swiftwind unitl I acctually equiped it. It did not really improve my DPS, leaving the tox axe in the main hand. I am guessing that this is a situational item, now, and I had SCHW for haste before my epic. It seems like I can kill mobs faster, solo, with the hand wraps and the tox/OLoS combo then I can with the tox/swifty combo. Have yet to try the OLoS/swifty combo because of the 30+ delay of the OLoS.

Oh well, I did 1/2 of it and am raiding PoH to get the SEoC so I will have both. I just wanted to let the rangers out there know this, so that if they have really nice weapons already, this probably wont be an upgrade for you. It is still a cool status symbol to have your epics. I am a lvl 52 woody ranger by the way, only took me a year to get this far, hehe.

▲ Page top
Faelin Bloodbriar and the Worn Note
QuoteReply
# Mar 28 2004 at 9:26 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593374729465
Scholar
167 posts
I read thru the posts and did see anything about Faelin Bloodbriar that I'm about to share. When I've seen her, she is always running around. Hailing her does not stop her for conversation. So I just ran behind her, gave the worn note (on the run) and quickly hit the Give button. No problems at all. I got her ring in return and she despawned. If you have experienced other different results, please let me know.

As to other posts on better Ranger-usable weapons, I think the idea is to foster a sense of pride and completion of a long and arduous quest. Anyone is able to collect enough plat to buy a sweet bazaar sold item.

In many ways, quests of this nature bring together several aspects of why we play the game. A sense of adventure, shared danger with comrades-in-arms, and the thrill when you achieve your final goal. After all, we are "Ever-Questing".
**steps down from soapbox**

Edited, Mon Mar 29 13:43:40 2004
▲ Page top

RE: sword

# Mar 03 2004 at 1:46 PM Rating: Decent
Aquendar
View User Forum
Scholar
195 posts
For those of us without access to the high end gear of the Elemental Planes and such, this is a GREAT Ranger sword for what is does for archery alone. It's very hard to get 40% and better haste items, let alone with Attack bonuses. MANY Rangers, even with better swords for melee, switch to Swiftwind when doing archery so they can get the attack bonus and get 40% haste. It still has great uses.

Also, this is still fairly comparable to almost every weapon we could buy in the bazaar, but with the haste . . .
____________________________
Farwarden Aquendar Lerilon, 105 Season Human Ranger of Tunare
Xegony
My Fantasy Novels
My Profile
▲ Page top
RE: sword
QuoteReply
# Mar 22 2004 at 8:14 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591788586634
Sage
531 posts
As far as I'm aware, regular worn haste items have NO effect on archery. If you want an item that effects archery you need to get yourself a good quiver. Quivers give 1/3 their weight reduction to bow haste. Thus, A Fleeting Quiver with 60% weight reduction will give you 20% bow haste.

Haste SPELLS on the other-hand will effect bow haste so get a Chanter, Shaman, or Bard(if grouped with them) to give you haste.

You would probably be better off with a good 36% haste item and better weapons than these swords. If money is a big problem, then these swords might be the upgrade you're looking for.
▲ Page top
RE: sword
QuoteReply
# Mar 22 2004 at 5:31 PM Rating: Excellent
Aquendar
View User Forum
Scholar
195 posts
Yes, worn haste DOES effect archery. They only gave us quiver haste (equal to 1/3 of the weight reduction) because our bows were, at the time, so badly underpowered.

Swiftwind is not the best weapon in the game, but for those us that will most likely never get to the really high tier stuff, it is GREAT! Haste, Attack, great stats and resists. While the damage/delay isn't as good as a Stained Axe, for example, the stained axe is most likely being used at 22-31% haste. Haste to 40% and this is comparable.

You say get 36% Haste item? Hmm, how about I buy one in the bazaar? Whoops, they are up for sale at 150K for plat-for-dollars cheaters to buy. Go kill something with one? Well, too high level for Naggy and Vox, guild not strong enough for some of the others. Yet, with help from friends we were able to get to Pegasus/Griffon Isle for this epic!

I have eyepatch of plunder as well (20% self spell haste) so I'm at 60% melee. With my Fleeting Quiver (60% WR, so 20% archery haste) I'm at 80% bow haste.

Please do your research before posting that worn haste has no effect on archery, like on a Ranger forum where this has been researched.

Let me also point out that if your spell and worn haste has already put you at your haste cap (like at 84% for me) archery is then added to it. So if I'm at 84% through worn and spell, I'm at 104% archery because of my fleeting quiver. It's true and documented by people much smarter than me!

Edited, Fri Apr 2 18:00:19 2004
____________________________
Farwarden Aquendar Lerilon, 105 Season Human Ranger of Tunare

Yes there are much better melee weaps for folks with Ele planes access. I STILL want to complete this for the +30 ATK for archery, and you need to complete Swiftwind to get the part needed to go with the Hate drop for Earthcaller. Earthcaller is a wonderfull solo weapon due to the SLOW. A Ele geared Ranger wouldn't use either epics all the time BUT they would still use both some of the time situationally. Thus its still worth the effort. I hope to get the Hole part done soon to make my Swiftwind

You can begin this epic at 46. Whether or not the NPCs will talk to you after that is based upon faction.
▲ Page top
Epic guides at EQRangers.com
QuoteReply
# Nov 17 2003 at 12:53 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591795128798
Scholar
60 posts
http://www.eqrangers.com/epic-text.htm is a nice step-by-step guide telling you who to talk to, where to get what, and who to give it to. http://www.eqrangers.com/epic-p.htm is a nice checklist (with auto-print Javascript) that lists each step. I suggest you print this out and check off each thing as you do it. You can do some things in a different order, but some items are used for intermediate tasks before being turned in. You might give away something you still need. Before you attempt a step out of order, make sure any turnin items aren't needed for other tasks.

Here's how I broke it up...

1-6 Getting the Shiny Tin Bowl
7-11 Getting the Hardened Mixture
12-16 Getting the Ancient Pattern
17-20 Getting the Platinum Speckled Powder
21-22 Getting the Enchanted Clay
23 Make the Runecrested Bowl
24-27 Getting the Ancient Longsword
28-34 Getting the Refined Mithril Blade
35-36 Getting Swiftwind
37-38 Getting Earthcaller

Obviously those can be broken down differently, but that seeemed pretty logical to me. Basically you have to do the whole epic minus the SEoC from Plane of Hate to get Swiftwind. Toward the end, the quests for the two items become intertwined, so that the only thing you can really skip to get just Swiftwind is the SEoC.

You give Faelin the Refined Ancient Sword to get Swiftwind. To spawn Faelin, you need to give Telin the sword first. You get that version of the sword from Usbak the Old. Usbak is spawned by giving Kinlo the hammer. That version of the hammer is from Mairee upon turning in the hammer off Jaeil and the SSoC from Plane of Air. The original version of the sword is from Telin in exchange for the Warmly Glowing Stone, which is from the stone off Venril Sathir's Remains and from the Runecrested Bowl. The bowl is from the Ancient Pattern, Platinum Speckled Powder, Enchanted Clay, and the Hardened Mixture. The Hardened Mixture is made in the Shiny Tin Bowl.

As you can see, you really have to do steps 1-36 to get your Swiftwind. The SSoC (and possibly other parts) could be MQed, but someone will have to do the first 36 steps to be able to get Swiftwind.
▲ Page top

RE: MQ turn in
QuoteReply
# Aug 07 2003 at 1:02 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592256912300
52 posts
I just did this last night, both myself and the person who was mq'ing the swirling sphere for me were dubious to the turnin-npc. She gave the sphere then I gave the dwarven smiths hammer and it was done...no faction needed apparently.
▲ Page top
RE: MQ turn in
QuoteReply
# Aug 10 2003 at 1:34 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255309664
22 posts
OK, but when he turns in his piece, does he hit give THEN i give my piece? Or do I do it while his give window still open?
In other worfs, does he do his WHOLE transaction before mine? i.e. he gives swirling sphere, then hits give, then I give hammer, then hit trade? Or does he give and keep window open while I give my piece? Then he hit give and I hit give? this is where I am confused.
▲ Page top
RE: MQ turn in
QuoteReply
# Aug 23 2003 at 2:04 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591767388859
78 posts
No, you don't have to turn it at the same time as the person who MQ it for you does. Let them turn it and then turn your part in.

Yesterday as I was crossing the Bazaar I /ooced that I was looking for a MQ Swirling Sphere of colour./ Someone who turned out to be a 57 epic (Swifty & Earthcaller) RNG told me he got the sphere and agreed on MQing it for a Tigeraptor Cloak and Long Sword of Ethereal Energy :-)

We then went to Mairee Silentone in Erudin Palace, he gave the sphere, told me he'd done so, then i gave the hammer, and Bob's your uncle. 15 minutes later i'd my epic in hand :) I then went and grouped in PoV to try it out, and it rules !

Many thanks to Andaeinn for his helping me getting Siwfty :)
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 23 2003 at 9:05 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) yay, got me swifty!
which is easier
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2003 at 3:51 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1595175499136
6 posts
which is easier to get swiftwind or earthcall and can just a lvl 57 get the stuff for it or kill all the stuff

▲ Page top
RE: which is easier
QuoteReply
# Jul 11 2003 at 9:40 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591785302887
View User Forum
6 posts
You have to complete the whole quest to get both earthcaller and swifty. Swifty is easier to get as it's all straight up fights/quests.

Earthcaller is a PITA as you need to get the SEOC from Hate which is an ultra rare drop.

So in order to get EC, you have to do all the steps of swifty. Then when you have swifty, EC only one drop from Hate away. It's getting that drop that will make you pull your hair out.

Edited, Fri Jul 11 10:33:51 2003

__DEL__1592259870396

▲ Page top
Turn Ins
QuoteReply
# Jun 05 2003 at 10:13 AM Rating: Decent
-REDACTED-
Scholar
42 posts
When you turn in the Hammer of the Ancients, do you get 2 "small bit of Mithril Ore" or does the first turn in mob give it back for the second turn in? Or is it required to repeat the steps to get another "Hammer of the Ancients"? I havent seen this posted anywhere else.
▲ Page top
RE: Turn Ins
QuoteReply
# Jul 11 2003 at 9:43 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591785302887
View User Forum
6 posts
You get one small piece of mithril ore. You give that small piece of mithril to Kinlo to get the refined mithril blade. You give the mob that spawns from the soulbound hammer the ancient sword and he gives you refined ancient sword.

Turn in refined ancient sword to Telin Darkforest. This will spawn Faelin Bloodbriar. You then giver the refined ancient sword, and you get swiftwind.
▲ Page top
Love it
QuoteReply
# May 23 2003 at 9:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591749747580
Scholar
34 posts

Swifty is great - A few minor things I have noticed that i would like to talk of / share .

Swifty has per haste of 40% - please remember there is a haste cap and a dly cap . Haste cap is quite low below 50 but not so relevant as youll be 50 ish when you get this. DLY cap .. no weapon can have its effective delay dropped below 10 ( this has been stated regularly by verant and soe) bear it in mind when choosing primary - It will take you a lot longer to get earthcaller but with this pairing soloing is great - 40% haste 50% slow and 500 dot means mob is a lot less scarey.

Im not getting into the ratio / dly pri/sec thing as people have already stated this correctly and if peeps dont get it they should have bought a finger waggler on ebay not a ranger :).

Some primary weapons to consider
Lupine dagger - Club of ice ocean - Long sword of ethereal energy - Edge of the nightwalker (<< my personal choice) - Snowchipper - all have advantages - good ratio , some proc , some have good stats , none are too slow . Quite a few are at the cheaper end of the market ( on a mature server so wont get into prices)

All in all swifty rules .... i love it :) the lightning on the blade ... the effort to get it all worth it and I am trying for earthcaller too - oh and soloing rangers ... eyepatch of plunder should get you to your haste limit as it is a spell :)

Ninjergranni Cazic Thule

5 posts
Actually the haste cap is 100%

# Jul 26 2003 at 11:51 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965820726
20 posts
No - the haste cap is dependant on level and only reaches 100% at 60. See http://cloud.prohosting.com/~talone/HasteData.htm for a full discussion.

34 posts
Ya ill try to find it .

It wasnt from a parser it was something to do with the actual game mechanics in that it ignored anything less then 10 dly and made it 10 due to a codding restriction I think
▲ Page top
RE: Love it
QuoteReply
# May 24 2003 at 7:19 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591787394073
6 posts
From what I've read there is no minimum delay cap. The way Everquest logs it only records once a second, so parses show the minimum delay to be 10 (1 second), even if you're hitting faster. I've never seen SoE stating there was a delay cap either - could you post a link? I'm interested to see if there really is a delay cap or not.
▲ Page top
RE: Love it
QuoteReply
# Jul 08 2003 at 4:20 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592252450297
Scholar
204 posts
I've searched and searched.. and no luck, but I also remember reading about a "delay cap" of 10. What i can't remember however is where i read this (most likely dev corner) or if it was referring to a hard cap including haste, or a minimum delay that would be placed on an item. Mosscovered twig is the fastest weapon i personally know of, and it would make sense if that's what they were referring to.

If i happen to be able to find the post itself, i'll put a link here.

Yes the Swirling Sphere of Color is MQable
QuoteReply
# Mar 04 2003 at 12:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591771529566
10 posts
Mariee Silentone was dubious to just about all my toons. Had a DE Rallos Zek warr MQ the sphere with my ranger, went fine. Just make sure you hand in the sphere first then the hammer that she gives you back, not the hammer that Kin-dude in Kaldim gives you.

MQ
QuoteReply
# Feb 08 2003 at 9:06 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591784866502
4 posts
Has anyone ever mq ed the Swirling sphere part of the quest if so any tricks to it?
▲ Page top
RE: MQ
QuoteReply
# Feb 10 2003 at 8:04 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592343499978
View User Forum
19 posts
In January, I had the Swirling Sphere MQ'ed for me.

Both my guild-leader and I were apprehensive to the NPC. Obviously, neither of us wanted to find out whether this mattered. So, my guild-leader put the Sphere into a trade window with the NPC, waited while I finished casting SupCamo on him, and then hit 'give'. Then I hit 'sneak', came up from behind, handed her my contibution and got my reward.

Again, whether we needed to do this is uncertain to me, but it seemed the safest bet.

Best of luck on your questing ... I hope this helps :)
▲ Page top
RE: MQ
QuoteReply
# Feb 13 2003 at 12:20 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757964404
9 posts
Got the MQ done with my ranger at dubious last night. As long as you're not KOS, you can get this done.
